zh.lifeSummary: 當Anne Payne於1621年7月31日之前出生在英國亨廷格多史爾素時，她的父親John Payne25歲，母親Anna Underhill24歲。她於1636年8月1日與Governor William Leete, Sr.在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾海爾 韋斯頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1668年9月1日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anne Payne于1621年7月31日之前出生在英国亨廷格多史爾素时，她的父亲John Payne25岁，母亲Anna Underhill24岁。她于1636年8月1日与Governor William Leete, Sr.在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾海爾 韋斯頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1668年9月1日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Anne Payne was born before 31 July 1621, in Southoe, Huntingdonshire, England, her father, John Payne, was 25 and her mother, Anna Underhill, was 24. She married Governor William Leete, Sr. on 1 August 1636, in Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died before 1 September 1668, in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anne Payne est née avant 31 juillet 1621, à Southoe, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, John Payne, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Anna Underhill, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Governor William Leete, Sr. le 1 août 1636, à Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée avant 1 septembre 1668, à Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterrée à Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anne Payne vor 31. Juli 1621 in Southoe, Huntingdonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Payne 25 und ihre Mutter Anna Underhill 24. Sie hat am 1. August 1636 in Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom Governor William Leete, Sr. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb vor 1. September 1668 in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anne Payne è nata il prima del 31 luglio 1621 nel Southoe, Huntingdonshire, England, sua padre, John Payne, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Anna Underhill, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Governor William Leete, Sr. il 1 agosto 1636, nel Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì prima del 1 settembre 1668, nel Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, e fu sepolta a Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne Payneが1621年7月31日以前にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアソウソで生まれた時，父John Payneは25歳，母Anna Underhillは24歳であった。1636年8月1日にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアヘイル ウエストンでGovernor William Leete, Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1668年9月1日以前にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベングイルフォードで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベングイルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Payne(이)가 1621년 7월 31일 이전 영국 헌틴그도실르 소우토에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Payne(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Underhill(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1636년 8월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 하일 웨스턴에서 Governor William Leete, Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1668년 9월 1일 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 길퍼드에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 길퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anne Payne nasceu antes 31 de julho de 1621, em Southoe, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Payne, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Anna Underhill, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Governor William Leete, Sr. em 1 de agosto de 1636, em Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 1 de setembro de 1668, em Guilford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, e foi sepultada em Guilford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anne Payne родилась перед 31 июля 1621 г. в местности соуто, хантингдоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Payne было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Underhill, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Governor William Leete, Sr. 1 августа 1636 г. в местности хеил вестон, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла перед 1 сентябрь 1668 г. в местности гуилфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика и была похоронена в местности гуилфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anne Payne nació antes de 31 de julio de 1621, en Southoe, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Payne, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Anna Underhill, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Governor William Leete, Sr. el 1 de agosto de 1636, en Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió antes de 1 de septiembre de 1668, en Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultada en Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
